_**Agent (gemini-2.5-pro 2025-09-16 01:54:58Z)**_

I've reviewed your `dot_zshrc.tmpl` and can see that you are loading `zsh-syntax-highlighting` as a plugin, but not `fast-syntax-highlighting`. Since `fast-syntax-highlighting` is a more performant alternative, I'd recommend using it and removing the other to avoid downloading an unused file.

I can also help you simplify your `.chezmoiexternal.toml` file. By renaming it to `.chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl`, we can use templating to reduce the repetitive code. Here's how the improved file would look:

```toml
{{- if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" }}
{{- $zshPlugin := dict "type" "archive" "exact" true "stripComponents" 1 "refreshPeriod" "168h" -}}

[".oh-my-zsh"]
    {{- toToml $zshPlugin | nindent 4 }}
    url = "https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/archive/master.tar.gz"

[".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/fast-syntax-highlighting"]
    {{- toToml $zshPlugin | nindent 4 }}
    url = "https://github.com/zdharma-continuum/fast-syntax-highlighting/archive/master.tar.gz"

[".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-autocomplete"]
    {{- toToml $zshPlugin | nindent 4 }}
    url = "https://github.com/marlonrichert/zsh-autocomplete/archive/master.tar.gz"

[".oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-autosuggestions"]
    {{- toToml $zshPlugin | nindent 4 }}
    url = "https://github.com/zsh-users/zsh-autosuggestions/archive/master.tar.gz"

[".oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/powerlevel10k"]
    {{- toToml $zshPlugin | nindent 4 }}
    url = "https://github.com/romkatv/powerlevel10k/archive/master.tar.gz"

[".local/share/zsh-llm-suggestions"]
    type = "git-repo"
    url = "https://github.com/cearley/zsh-llm-suggestions.git"
    exact = true
    refreshPeriod = "24h"

[".local/share/zsh-functions"]
    type = "git-repo"
    url = "https://github.com/cearley/zsh-functions.git"
    exact = true
    refreshPeriod = "24h"

{{- if has "dev" .tags }}
[".jenv"]
    type = "git-repo"
    url = "https://github.com/jenv/jenv.git"
    exact = true
    refreshPeriod = "168h"
{{- end }}
{{- end }}
```

In this version, I've also replaced `zsh-syntax-highlighting` with `fast-syntax-highlighting`.

As a general best practice, I'd also recommend pinning your external dependencies to specific versions or tags instead of pulling from the `master` branch. This will make your setup more stable and predictable.

Overall, you have a very well-structured and impressive `chezmoi` configuration!

Would you like me to apply these changes for you?

<pre><code>Content from @/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoiscripts/run_once_after_darwin-15-install-azul-zulu-jdk.sh.tmpl:
{{- if and (eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34;) (has &#34;dev&#34; .tags) -}}
#!/bin/bash

# Azul Zulu JDK installation script for macOS development environments
# Only runs when &#39;dev&#39; tag is present
set -euo pipefail

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

# Constants
readonly JAVA_INSTALL_DIR=&#34;/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines&#34;
readonly TEMP_DIR=&#34;/tmp/azul-jdk-install&#34;
readonly API_BASE_URL=&#34;https://api.azul.com/metadata/v1/zulu/packages&#34;

# Architecture mapping for Azul downloads
{{- if eq .chezmoi.arch &#34;amd64&#34; }}
readonly ARCH=&#34;x64&#34;
{{- else if eq .chezmoi.arch &#34;arm64&#34; }}
readonly ARCH=&#34;aarch64&#34;
{{- else }}
readonly ARCH=&#34;x64&#34;  # fallback to x64
{{- end }}

# Script-specific functions

# Check if JDK is already installed
check_existing_jdk() {
    if [ -d &#34;$JAVA_INSTALL_DIR&#34; ] &amp;&amp; [ &#34;$(ls -1 &#34;$JAVA_INSTALL_DIR&#34; 2&gt;/dev/null | grep -c &#34;zulu&#34;)&#34; -gt 0 ]; then
        local existing_jdk
        existing_jdk=$(ls -1 &#34;$JAVA_INSTALL_DIR&#34; | grep &#34;zulu&#34; | head -1)
        print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Found existing Azul Zulu JDK: $existing_jdk&#34;
        
        # Check if it&#39;s a recent version (Java 17+ LTS)
        if echo &#34;$existing_jdk&#34; | grep -qE &#34;(zulu-?1[789]|zulu-?2[0-9])&#34;; then
            print_message &#34;skip&#34; &#34;Recent Azul Zulu JDK already installed, skipping installation&#34;
            return 0
        else
            print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Older JDK version found, will install latest LTS version&#34;
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    return 1
}

# Get latest LTS JDK download URL
get_latest_lts_url() {
    local api_url=&#34;${API_BASE_URL}?os=macosx&amp;arch=${ARCH}&amp;java_package_type=jdk&amp;archive_type=tar.gz&amp;support_term=lts&amp;latest=true&#34;
    
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Fetching latest LTS JDK information for architecture: $ARCH&#34;
    
    # Use curl to fetch the API response
    local response
    if ! response=$(curl -s &#34;$api_url&#34;); then
        print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;Failed to fetch JDK information from Azul API&#34;
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if we got a valid response
    if [ -z &#34;$response&#34; ]; then
        print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;Empty response from Azul API&#34;
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Extract download URL for standard JDK (ca variant, not ca-fx or ca-crac)
    # Filter for entries that contain &#34;-ca-jdk&#34; but not &#34;-ca-fx&#34; or &#34;-ca-crac&#34;
    local download_url
    if ! download_url=$(echo &#34;$response&#34; | grep -o &#39;&#34;download_url&#34;:&#34;[^&#34;]*-ca-jdk[^&#34;]*&#39; | grep -v -- &#39;-ca-fx\|-ca-crac&#39; | head -1 | sed &#39;s/&#34;download_url&#34;:&#34;//&#39;); then
        print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;Failed to parse download URL from API response&#34;
        return 1
    fi
    
    if [ -z &#34;$download_url&#34; ]; then
        print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;No download URL found for architecture: $ARCH&#34;
        return 1
    fi
    
    
    echo &#34;$download_url&#34;
}

# Download and verify JDK
download_jdk() {
    local download_url=&#34;$1&#34;
    local filename
    filename=$(basename &#34;$download_url&#34;)
    local download_path=&#34;${TEMP_DIR}/${filename}&#34;
    
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Downloading: $filename&#34;
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;URL: $download_url&#34;
    
    # Create temp directory
    ensure_directory &#34;$TEMP_DIR&#34;
    
    # Download JDK
    if ! download_file &#34;$download_url&#34; &#34;$download_path&#34; &#34;$filename&#34;; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    echo &#34;$download_path&#34;
}

# Install JDK
install_jdk() {
    local download_path=&#34;$1&#34;
    
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Installing JDK to $JAVA_INSTALL_DIR&#34;
    
    # Ensure install directory exists
    ensure_directory &#34;$JAVA_INSTALL_DIR&#34; true
    
    # Extract to temp location first
    local extract_temp=&#34;${TEMP_DIR}/extract&#34;
    ensure_directory &#34;$extract_temp&#34;
    
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Extracting JDK archive...&#34;
    if ! tar -xzf &#34;$download_path&#34; -C &#34;$extract_temp&#34;; then
        print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;Failed to extract JDK archive&#34;
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Find the extracted JDK directory (.jdk file inside the extracted folder)
    local jdk_dir
    jdk_dir=$(find &#34;$extract_temp&#34; -name &#34;*.jdk&#34; -type d | head -1)
    
    if [ -z &#34;$jdk_dir&#34; ] || [ ! -d &#34;$jdk_dir&#34; ]; then
        print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;Could not find extracted .jdk directory&#34;
        return 1
    fi
    
    local jdk_name
    jdk_name=$(basename &#34;$jdk_dir&#34;)
    local final_path=&#34;${JAVA_INSTALL_DIR}/${jdk_name}&#34;
    
    # Move to final location
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Moving JDK to final location: $final_path&#34;
    if ! sudo mv &#34;$jdk_dir&#34; &#34;$final_path&#34;; then
        print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;Failed to move JDK to installation directory&#34;
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Set proper permissions
    sudo chown -R root:wheel &#34;$final_path&#34;
    sudo chmod -R 755 &#34;$final_path&#34;
    
    print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;JDK installed successfully to: $final_path&#34;
    
    # Verify installation
    if [ -x &#34;$final_path/Contents/Home/bin/java&#34; ]; then
        local java_version
        java_version=$(&#34;$final_path/Contents/Home/bin/java&#34; -version 2&gt;&amp;1 | head -1)
        print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;Java version: $java_version&#34;
        
        # Add to jenv if available
        add_to_jenv &#34;$final_path/Contents/Home&#34;
    else
        print_message &#34;warning&#34; &#34;Java binary not found or not executable&#34;
    fi
}

# Add JDK to jenv if available
add_to_jenv() {
    local java_home=&#34;$1&#34;
    
    # Check if jenv is available
    if ! command_exists jenv; then
        print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;jenv not found, skipping jenv configuration&#34;
        return 0
    fi
    
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Adding JDK to jenv: $java_home&#34;
    
    # Check if this Java version is already in jenv
    if jenv versions | grep -q &#34;$(basename &#34;$(dirname &#34;$java_home&#34;)&#34;)&#34;; then
        print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;JDK already configured in jenv&#34;
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Add to jenv
    if jenv add &#34;$java_home&#34;; then
        print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;JDK added to jenv successfully&#34;
        
        # Get the version that was added
        local jenv_version
        jenv_version=$(jenv versions | grep &#34;$(basename &#34;$(dirname &#34;$java_home&#34;)&#34;)&#34; | awk &#39;{print $1}&#39; | head -1)
        
        if [ -n &#34;$jenv_version&#34; ]; then
            print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Available jenv version: $jenv_version&#34;
            print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;To use this version globally: jenv global $jenv_version&#34;
            print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;To use this version locally: jenv local $jenv_version&#34;
        fi
    else
        print_message &#34;warning&#34; &#34;Failed to add JDK to jenv&#34;
    fi
}

# Cleanup temp files
cleanup() {
    cleanup_temp_dir &#34;$TEMP_DIR&#34;
}

# Main function
main() {
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Running Azul Zulu JDK installation (dev tag detected)&#34;
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Target architecture: $ARCH&#34;
    
    # Set trap to cleanup on exit
    trap cleanup EXIT
    
    # Check if JDK is already installed
    if check_existing_jdk; then
        exit 0
    fi
    
    # Check prerequisites
    if ! require_tools curl tar; then
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Get download URL
    local download_url
    if ! download_url=$(get_latest_lts_url); then
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Download JDK
    local download_path
    if ! download_path=$(download_jdk &#34;$download_url&#34;); then
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Install JDK
    if ! install_jdk &#34;$download_path&#34;; then
        exit 1
    fi
    
    print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;Azul Zulu JDK installation completed successfully!&#34;
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;You may need to restart your terminal or source your shell profile&#34;
    
    if command_exists jenv; then
        print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;JDK has been added to jenv. Use &#39;jenv versions&#39; to see available versions&#34;
        print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Use &#39;jenv global &lt;version&gt;&#39; or &#39;jenv local &lt;version&gt;&#39; to set Java version&#34;
    else
        print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;To set JAVA_HOME manually, add this to your shell profile:&#34;
        echo &#34;export JAVA_HOME=\$(ls -1 $JAVA_INSTALL_DIR | grep zulu | head -1 | xargs -I {} echo \&#34;$JAVA_INSTALL_DIR/{}/Contents/Home\&#34;)&#34;
    fi
}

# Run main function with all arguments
main &#34;$@&#34;
{{ end -}}
Content from @/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoiscripts/run_once_after_darwin-40-initialize-conda.sh.tmpl:
{{- if and (eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34;) (has &#34;dev&#34; .tags) -}}
#!/bin/bash

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

# Check required tools
if ! require_tools conda; then
    exit 1
fi

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Initializing conda for zsh shell...&#34;
conda init zsh

{{- if and (eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34;) .microsoft_email -}}
#!/bin/bash

set -eufo pipefail

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Setting up Microsoft Defender for Individuals...&#34;

# Check if Microsoft Defender is already installed
if is_app_installed &#34;/Applications/Microsoft Defender.app&#34;; then
    print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;Microsoft Defender is already installed&#34;
    exit 0
fi

# Open the Microsoft Defender download page
print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Opening Microsoft Defender download page in your default browser...&#34;
open &#34;https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/microsoft-defender-for-individuals#Download-the-app&#34;

echo &#34;&#34;
print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Manual Installation Instructions:&#34;
echo &#34;1. Click &#39;Download for MacOS&#39; on the page that just opened&#34;
echo &#34;2. Once downloaded, open the installer package&#34;
echo &#34;3. Follow the installation wizard to install Microsoft Defender&#34;
echo &#34;4. After installation, launch Microsoft Defender&#34;
echo &#34;5. Sign in with your Microsoft account: {{ .microsoft_email }}&#34;
echo &#34;&#34;

# Wait a moment for the page to load, then provide additional guidance
sleep 3

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Additional Setup Tips:&#34;
echo &#34;- Microsoft Defender may require Full Disk Access permissions&#34;
echo &#34;- You can grant permissions in System Preferences &gt; Security &amp; Privacy &gt; Privacy &gt; Full Disk Access&#34;
echo &#34;- Add Microsoft Defender to the list of allowed applications&#34;
echo &#34;&#34;

# Wait for the user to complete the installation
if wait_for_app_installation &#34;/Applications/Microsoft Defender.app&#34; &#34;Microsoft Defender&#34;; then
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Don&#39;t forget to sign in with your Microsoft account: {{ .microsoft_email }}&#34;
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Grant Full Disk Access permissions if prompted for complete protection&#34;
else
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Microsoft Defender can be manually installed from: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/microsoft-defender-for-individuals&#34;
    exit 1
fi

{{- if and (eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34;) (has &#34;ai&#34; .tags) -}}
#!/bin/bash

set -eufo pipefail

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Setting up Claude Desktop App...&#34;

# Check if Claude Desktop is already installed
if is_app_installed &#34;/Applications/Claude.app&#34;; then
    print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;Claude Desktop is already installed&#34;
    exit 0
fi

# Open the Claude Desktop download page
print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Opening Claude Desktop download page in your default browser...&#34;
open &#34;https://claude.ai/download&#34;

echo &#34;&#34;
print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Manual Installation Instructions:&#34;
echo &#34;1. Click &#39;Download for Mac&#39; on the page that just opened&#34;
echo &#34;2. Once downloaded, open the downloaded .dmg file&#34;
echo &#34;3. Drag Claude.app to your Applications folder&#34;
echo &#34;4. After installation, launch Claude from your Applications folder&#34;
echo &#34;5. Sign in with your Anthropic account or create one if needed&#34;
echo &#34;&#34;

# Wait a moment for the page to load, then provide additional guidance
sleep 3

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Additional Setup Tips:&#34;
echo &#34;- Claude Desktop may request permissions for notifications and accessibility&#34;
echo &#34;- You can manage permissions in System Preferences &gt; Security &amp; Privacy&#34;
echo &#34;- Claude Desktop works best when allowed to run in the background&#34;
echo &#34;- You can pin Claude to your Dock for easy access&#34;
echo &#34;&#34;

# Wait for the user to complete the installation
if wait_for_app_installation &#34;/Applications/Claude.app&#34; &#34;Claude Desktop&#34;; then
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;You can now launch Claude from your Applications folder&#34;
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Sign in with your Anthropic account to start using Claude Desktop&#34;
else
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Claude Desktop can be manually installed from: https://claude.ai/download&#34;
    exit 1
fi

{{- if and (eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34;) (has &#34;ai&#34; .tags) -}}
#!/bin/bash

# Install Basic Memory MCP server for Claude Desktop
# Prerequisite: uv (installed by run_once_before_darwin-15-install-uv.sh.tmpl)

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

# Check required tools
if ! require_tools curl uvx; then
    exit 1
fi

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Installing Basic Memory MCP server for Claude Desktop...&#34;

# Skip if basic-memory is already installed.
if uvx basic-memory --version &gt;/dev/null 2&gt;&amp;1; then
    print_message &#34;skip&#34; &#34;Skipping basic-memory installation: basic-memory is already installed&#34;
    exit 0
fi

# Install Basic Memory via the recommended installer
# This will install uv (if not already installed), install Basic Memory via uv,
# and configure Claude Desktop automatically with user permission.
curl -LsSf https://basicmemory.com/install/latest.sh | sh

print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;Basic Memory MCP server installation completed&#34;

Content from @/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoiscripts/run_once_after_darwin-85-configure-system-defaults.sh.tmpl:
{{- if eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34; -}}
#!/bin/bash

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

# Check required tools
if ! require_tools defaults /usr/libexec/PlistBuddy; then
    exit 1
fi

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Configuring macOS system defaults...&#34;

{{- if has &#34;iterm2&#34; .packages.darwin.core.casks }}
# iTerm2: Enable &#39;Close windows when quitting an application&#39;
defaults write com.googlecode.iterm2 NSQuitAlwaysKeepsWindows -bool true

# iTerm2: Disable &#39;Clicking on a command selects it to restrict Find and Filter&#39;
defaults write com.googlecode.iterm2 ClickToSelectCommand -bool false

# iTerm2: Load settings from custom folder
defaults write com.googlecode.iterm2 PrefsCustomFolder -string &#34;~/.dotfiles/iTerm/settings&#34;
defaults write com.googlecode.iterm2 LoadPrefsFromCustomFolder -bool false

# iTerm2: Save changes when quitting
defaults write com.googlecode.iterm2 NoSyncNeverRemindPrefsChangesLostForFile -bool true
defaults write com.googlecode.iterm2 NoSyncNeverRemindPrefsChangesLostForFile_selection -int 2

# iTerm2: Set default profile font to MesloLGSNF size 13
/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c &#34;Set &#39;New Bookmarks&#39;:0:&#39;Normal Font&#39; &#39;MesloLGSNF-Regular 13&#39;&#34; ~/Library/Preferences/com.googlecode.iterm2.plist 2&gt;/dev/null || true
{{- end }}

print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;System defaults configured successfully&#34;
{{ end -}}

#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

# Check required tools
if ! require_tools curl; then
    exit 1

    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Rust is already installed. Updating...&#34;

curl --proto &#39;=https&#39; --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Adding Rust to shell profile...&#34;
(
    echo
    echo &#39;source &#34;$HOME/.cargo/env&#34;&#39;
) &gt;&gt;&#34;$HOME&#34;/.zprofile

print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;Rust installed successfully!&#34;

{{- if eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34; -}}
#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

# Check required tools
if ! require_tools curl; then
    exit 1
fi

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Installing uv - Python package manager...&#34;
print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Using standalone installer from astral.sh&#34;

# Exit immediately if uv is already in $PATH
if command_exists uv; then
    print_message &#34;skip&#34; &#34;uv is already installed&#34;
    exit 0
fi

# Install uv - Python package manager
print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Downloading and installing uv...&#34;
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Setting up shell completions...&#34;

# Enable shell autocompletion for bash
if [ -f ~/.bashrc ] &amp;&amp; ! grep -q &#34;uv generate-shell-completion bash&#34; ~/.bashrc; then
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Adding bash completion for uv&#34;
    echo &#39;eval &#34;$(uv generate-shell-completion bash)&#34;&#39; &gt;&gt; ~/.bashrc
fi
if [ -f ~/.bashrc ] &amp;&amp; ! grep -q &#34;uvx --generate-shell-completion bash&#34; ~/.bashrc; then
    echo &#39;eval &#34;$(uvx --generate-shell-completion bash)&#34;&#39; &gt;&gt; ~/.bashrc
fi

# Enable shell autocompletion for zsh
if [ -f ~/.zshrc ] &amp;&amp; ! grep -q &#34;uv generate-shell-completion zsh&#34; ~/.zshrc; then
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Adding zsh completion for uv&#34;
    echo &#39;eval &#34;$(uv generate-shell-completion zsh)&#34;&#39; &gt;&gt; ~/.zshrc
fi
if [ -f ~/.zshrc ] &amp;&amp; ! grep -q &#34;uvx --generate-shell-completion zsh&#34; ~/.zshrc; then
    echo &#39;eval &#34;$(uvx --generate-shell-completion zsh)&#34;&#39; &gt;&gt; ~/.zshrc
fi

print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;uv installed successfully!&#34;
print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;You may need to restart your terminal or source your shell profile&#34;

{{ end -}}
Content from @/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoiscripts/run_once_before_darwin-35-install-nvm.sh.tmpl:
{{- if and (eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34;) (has &#34;dev&#34; .tags) -}}
#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

# Check required tools
if ! require_tools curl grep cut; then
    exit 1
fi

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Installing nvm (Node Version Manager)...&#34;

# nvm installs to $HOME/.nvm by default.
# If this directory exists, we assume nvm is installed.
# Note that `command -v nvm` doesn&#39;t find it in a fresh
# shell context because it&#39;s a shell function, not a binary.
if [ -d &#34;$HOME/.nvm&#34; ]; then
    print_message &#34;skip&#34; &#34;nvm is already installed&#34;
    exit 0
fi

# Get the latest nvm version from GitHub API
print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Fetching latest nvm version from GitHub...&#34;
NVM_VERSION=$(curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/nvm-sh/nvm/releases/latest | grep &#39;&#34;tag_name&#34;&#39; | cut -d &#39;&#34;&#39; -f 4)

if [ -z &#34;$NVM_VERSION&#34; ]; then
    print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;Failed to fetch latest nvm version&#34;
    exit 1
fi

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Installing nvm $NVM_VERSION...&#34;

# Install nvm using the official installation script
print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Downloading and running nvm installation script...&#34;
curl -o- &#34;https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/$NVM_VERSION/install.sh&#34; | bash

# Verify installation
if [ -d &#34;$HOME/.nvm&#34; ]; then
    print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;nvm installed successfully!&#34;
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;You may need to restart your terminal or run &#39;source ~/.zshrc&#39; to use nvm&#34;
else
    print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;nvm installation failed&#34;
    exit 1
fi
{{ end -}}
Content from @/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_darwin-45-setup-github-auth.sh.tmpl:
{{- if and (eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34;) .has_keepassxc_db -}}
#!/bin/bash

# Chezmoi script to configure GitHub authentication.
# This script runs whenever the GitHub token changes, after chezmoi applies other changes on macOS.

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

# Check required tools
if ! require_tools git docker gh; then
    exit 1
fi

set -e

GITHUB_USERNAME={{ .gh_username | quote }}
GITHUB_TOKEN={{ keepassxcAttribute &#34;GitHub&#34; &#34;Access Token&#34; | quote }}

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Setting up GitHub authentication...&#34;

setup_git_credentials() {
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Configuring git credential helper&#34;
    
    # Set up git credential helper to cache credentials
    git config --global credential.helper cache
    
    # Configure git to use the token for GitHub
    git config --global credential.https://github.com.username &#34;$GITHUB_USERNAME&#34;
    
    print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;Git credential helper configured&#34;
}

setup_ghcr_login() {
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Logging into GitHub Container Registry (GHCR)&#34;
    
    # Login to GHCR using the token
    echo &#34;$GITHUB_TOKEN&#34; | docker login ghcr.io --username &#34;$GITHUB_USERNAME&#34; --password-stdin
    
    print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;Successfully logged into GHCR&#34;
}

setup_gh_cli() {
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Configuring GitHub CLI authentication&#34;
    
    # Set up gh CLI authentication using the token
    echo &#34;$GITHUB_TOKEN&#34; | gh auth login --with-token
    
    print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;GitHub CLI authentication configured&#34;
}

main() {
    # Set up git credentials
    setup_git_credentials
    
    # Set up GHCR login
    setup_ghcr_login
    
    # Set up GitHub CLI
    setup_gh_cli
    
    print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;GitHub authentication setup completed successfully&#34;
}

main &#34;$@&#34;

{{ end -}}
Content from @/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_darwin-88-setup-chronosync-symlinks.sh.tmpl:
{{- if and (eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34;) (stat &#34;/Applications/ChronoSync.app&#34;) -}}
#!/bin/bash

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

{{ $computerName := output &#34;scutil&#34; &#34;--get&#34; &#34;ComputerName&#34; | trim -}}
{{ $documentsTasksDir := joinPath .chezmoi.homeDir &#34;Documents/ChronoSync&#34; $computerName &#34;Tasks&#34; -}}
{{ $documentsTemplatesDir := joinPath .chezmoi.homeDir &#34;Documents/ChronoSync/Templates&#34; -}}

# Tasks directory contents hash: {{ if stat $documentsTasksDir }}{{ output &#34;sh&#34; &#34;-c&#34; (printf &#34;find &#39;%s&#39; -type f -exec basename &#39;{}&#39; \\; | sort | sha256sum&#34; $documentsTasksDir) }}{{ else }}empty{{ end }}
# Templates directory contents hash: {{ if stat $documentsTemplatesDir }}{{ output &#34;sh&#34; &#34;-c&#34; (printf &#34;find &#39;%s&#39; -type f -exec basename &#39;{}&#39; \\; | sort | sha256sum&#34; $documentsTemplatesDir) }}{{ else }}empty{{ end }}

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Setting up ChronoSync symbolic links...&#34;

# Define required directories
APP_SUPPORT_TASKS=&#34;$HOME/Library/Application Support/ChronoSync/Tasks&#34;
APP_SUPPORT_TEMPLATES=&#34;$HOME/Library/Application Support/ChronoSync/Templates&#34;
{{ $computerName := output &#34;scutil&#34; &#34;--get&#34; &#34;ComputerName&#34; | trim }}
DOCUMENTS_TASKS=&#34;$HOME/Documents/ChronoSync/{{- $computerName -}}/Tasks&#34;
DOCUMENTS_TEMPLATES=&#34;$HOME/Documents/ChronoSync/Templates&#34;

# Check if all required directories exist
if [ ! -d &#34;$APP_SUPPORT_TASKS&#34; ]; then
    print_message &#34;skip&#34; &#34;Skipping ChronoSync setup: $APP_SUPPORT_TASKS not found&#34;
    exit 0
fi

if [ ! -d &#34;$APP_SUPPORT_TEMPLATES&#34; ]; then
    print_message &#34;skip&#34; &#34;Skipping ChronoSync setup: $APP_SUPPORT_TEMPLATES not found&#34;
    exit 0
fi

ensure_directory &#34;$DOCUMENTS_TASKS&#34;
ensure_directory &#34;$DOCUMENTS_TEMPLATES&#34;

print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;All required directories found&#34;

# Function to create symbolic link with checks
create_symlink() {
    local source=&#34;$1&#34;
    local target=&#34;$2&#34;
    local filename=&#34;$3&#34;
    
    if [ -f &#34;$source&#34; ]; then
        if [ -L &#34;$target&#34; ] &amp;&amp; [ &#34;$(readlink &#34;$target&#34;)&#34; = &#34;$source&#34; ]; then
            print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;Symlink already exists and is correct: $filename&#34;
        else
            # Remove existing file/link if it exists
            [ -e &#34;$target&#34; ] &amp;&amp; rm &#34;$target&#34;
            ln -s &#34;$source&#34; &#34;$target&#34;
            print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Created symlink: $filename&#34;
        fi
    else
        print_message &#34;warning&#34; &#34;Source file not found: $source&#34;
    fi
}

# Create symbolic links for all files in Tasks directory
print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Processing ChronoSync Tasks...&#34;
for source_file in &#34;$DOCUMENTS_TASKS&#34;/*; do
    # Skip if no files match the pattern
    [ -e &#34;$source_file&#34; ] || continue
    
    # Get just the filename
    filename=$(basename &#34;$source_file&#34;)
    target_file=&#34;$APP_SUPPORT_TASKS/$filename&#34;
    
    create_symlink &#34;$source_file&#34; &#34;$target_file&#34; &#34;$filename&#34;
done

# Create symbolic links for all files in Templates directory
print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Processing ChronoSync Templates...&#34;
for source_file in &#34;$DOCUMENTS_TEMPLATES&#34;/*; do
    # Skip if no files match the pattern
    [ -e &#34;$source_file&#34; ] || continue
    
    # Get just the filename
    filename=$(basename &#34;$source_file&#34;)
    target_file=&#34;$APP_SUPPORT_TEMPLATES/$filename&#34;
    
    create_symlink &#34;$source_file&#34; &#34;$target_file&#34; &#34;$filename&#34;
done

print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;ChronoSync symbolic links setup completed&#34;
{{ end -}}
Content from @/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_darwin-90-update-hosts.sh.tmpl:
{{- if eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34; -}}
{{- $hostsDataFile := joinPath .chezmoi.homeDir &#34;.hosts-data.json&#34; -}}
#!/bin/bash

# Chezmoi script to update /etc/hosts with entries from ~/.hosts-data.json.
# The JSON structure is expected to be:
# {
#   &#34;hosts&#34;: [
#     {&#34;address&#34;: &#34;127.0.0.1&#34;, &#34;name&#34;: &#34;example.local&#34;},
#     {&#34;address&#34;: &#34;10.0.0.1&#34;, &#34;name&#34;: &#34;router.local&#34;}
#   ]
# }
# This script runs whenever the hosts data changes, after chezmoi applies other changes on macOS.

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

# Check required tools
if ! require_tools mktemp grep sed cp mv; then
    exit 1
fi

set -e

HOSTS_FILE=&#34;/etc/hosts&#34;
BACKUP_FILE=&#34;/etc/hosts.chezmoi.orig&#34;
SEPARATOR_COMMENT=&#34;# chezmoi-managed entries - do not edit manually&#34;
HOSTS_DATA_FILE={{ $hostsDataFile | quote }}

{{- $hostsData := dict }}
{{- if stat $hostsDataFile }}
{{- $hostsData = include $hostsDataFile | fromJson }}
# HOSTS_FILE hash: {{ include $hostsDataFile | sha256sum }}
{{- else }}
# No hosts data file present
{{- end }}

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Updating $HOSTS_FILE...&#34;

create_host_entries() {
    # Create a temporary file to store the parsed entries
    TEMP_ENTRIES=$(mktemp)
    
    # Use .hosts-data.json data to generate host entries
{{- if stat $hostsDataFile }}
  {{- if hasKey $hostsData &#34;hosts&#34; }}
    {{- range $hostsData.hosts }}
    echo &#34;{{ .address }} {{ .name }}&#34; &gt;&gt; &#34;$TEMP_ENTRIES&#34;
    {{- end }}
  {{- end }}
{{- end }}

    # Check if we got any entries
    if [ ! -s &#34;$TEMP_ENTRIES&#34; ]; then
        print_message &#34;warning&#34; &#34;No host entries found in $HOSTS_DATA_FILE&#34;
        rm -f &#34;$TEMP_ENTRIES&#34;
        echo &#34;&#34;
        return 1
    fi
    
    echo &#34;$TEMP_ENTRIES&#34;
}

backup_hosts() {
    if [ ! -f &#34;$BACKUP_FILE&#34; ]; then
        print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Creating backup of $HOSTS_FILE to $BACKUP_FILE&#34;
        sudo cp &#34;$HOSTS_FILE&#34; &#34;$BACKUP_FILE&#34;
    fi
}

update_hosts() {
    local temp_entries_file=&#34;$1&#34;
    
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Updating $HOSTS_FILE with entries from $HOSTS_DATA_FILE&#34;
    
    TEMP_HOSTS=$(mktemp)
    
    # Start with the current hosts file, removing any existing chezmoi-managed entries
    sudo grep -v &#34;# chezmoi-managed&#34; &#34;$HOSTS_FILE&#34; &gt; &#34;$TEMP_HOSTS&#34; || true
    
    # Add a separator comment
    echo &#34;&#34; &gt;&gt; &#34;$TEMP_HOSTS&#34;
    echo &#34;$SEPARATOR_COMMENT&#34; &gt;&gt; &#34;$TEMP_HOSTS&#34;
    
    # Add the new entries
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        echo &#34;$line # chezmoi-managed&#34; &gt;&gt; &#34;$TEMP_HOSTS&#34;
    done &lt; &#34;$temp_entries_file&#34;
    
    # Replace the original hosts file
    sudo mv &#34;$TEMP_HOSTS&#34; &#34;$HOSTS_FILE&#34;
    
    # Clean up
    rm -f &#34;$temp_entries_file&#34;
}

show_changes() {
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Current chezmoi-managed entries in $HOSTS_FILE:&#34;
    sudo grep &#34;# chezmoi-managed&#34; &#34;$HOSTS_FILE&#34; | grep -v &#34;$SEPARATOR_COMMENT&#34; | sed &#39;s/# chezmoi-managed//&#39; || print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;No chezmoi-managed entries found&#34;
}

remove_chezmoi_entries() {
    if sudo grep -q &#34;# chezmoi-managed&#34; &#34;$HOSTS_FILE&#34;; then
        backup_hosts
        TEMP_HOSTS=$(mktemp)
        sudo grep -v &#34;# chezmoi-managed&#34; &#34;$HOSTS_FILE&#34; &gt; &#34;$TEMP_HOSTS&#34;
        sudo mv &#34;$TEMP_HOSTS&#34; &#34;$HOSTS_FILE&#34;
        print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;Removed all chezmoi-managed entries from $HOSTS_FILE&#34;
    else
        print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;No chezmoi-managed entries found to remove&#34;
    fi
}

main() {
    # Check if hosts data file exists and if we have entries to clean up
    if [ ! -f &#34;$HOSTS_DATA_FILE&#34; ] &amp;&amp; sudo grep -q &#34;# chezmoi-managed&#34; &#34;$HOSTS_FILE&#34;; then
        print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;No hosts data file found at $HOSTS_DATA_FILE, removing chezmoi-managed entries&#34;
        remove_chezmoi_entries
        exit 0
    elif [ ! -f &#34;$HOSTS_DATA_FILE&#34; ]; then
        print_message &#34;skip&#34; &#34;No hosts data file found at $HOSTS_DATA_FILE&#34;
        exit 0
    fi
    
    # Create host entries from template data
    if ! temp_entries_file=$(create_host_entries); then
        print_message &#34;skip&#34; &#34;No host entries to process, exiting&#34;
        exit 0
    fi
    
    # Ensure temp file exists
    if [ ! -f &#34;$temp_entries_file&#34; ]; then
        print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;Temporary entries file not found: $temp_entries_file&#34;
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Backup the current hosts file
    backup_hosts
    
    # Update the hosts file
    update_hosts &#34;$temp_entries_file&#34;
    
    # Show the changes
    show_changes
    
    print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;$HOSTS_FILE update completed successfully&#34;
}

main &#34;$@&#34;

{{ end -}}

{{- if eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34; -}}
#!/bin/bash

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

# Check required tools
if ! require_tools brew; then
    exit 1
fi

# syncthing version: {{ output &#34;syncthing&#34; &#34;--version&#34; }}

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Restarting syncthing service after syncthing update...&#34;

# Check if syncthing service is loaded in brew services
if brew services list | grep -q &#34;syncthing&#34;; then
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Restarting syncthing service...&#34;
    brew services restart syncthing
    
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;syncthing service restarted successfully&#34;
    else
        print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;Failed to restart syncthing service&#34;
        exit 1
    fi
else
    print_message &#34;warning&#34; &#34;syncthing service not found in brew services - skipping restart&#34;
    exit 0
fi

{{- if eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34; -}}
#!/bin/sh

# SSH GitHub connectivity test

set -euo pipefail

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

{{/* Hash all SSH private keys to trigger re-execution when they change */}}
{{- $sshDir := joinPath .chezmoi.homeDir &#34;.ssh&#34; }}
{{- $sshKeys := glob (joinPath $sshDir &#34;id_*&#34;) }}
{{- $privateKeys := list }}
{{- range $sshKeys }}
  {{- if not (hasSuffix &#34;.pub&#34; .) }}
    {{- $privateKeys = append $privateKeys . }}
  {{- end }}
{{- end }}
{{- if $privateKeys }}
# SSH keys hash: {{ range $privateKeys }}{{ . | sha256sum }}{{ end }}
{{- else }}
# SSH keys hash: no-ssh-keys-found
{{- end }}

readonly GITHUB_SSH_HOST=&#34;ssh://git@github.com&#34;
readonly SSH_SUCCESS_EXIT_CODE=1  # GitHub returns 1 on successful auth without shell access
readonly SSH_TIMEOUT=10
readonly SSH_CONNECT_TIMEOUT=5

# Check if common SSH key files exist
check_ssh_keys() {
    if [ ! -d &#34;$HOME/.ssh&#34; ]; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check for common SSH key types (private keys only)
    for key_type in ed25519 rsa ecdsa dsa; do
        key_path=&#34;$HOME/.ssh/id_${key_type}&#34;
        if [ -f &#34;$key_path&#34; ]; then
            # Verify it&#39;s a private key (not a .pub file)
            if [ &#34;$(basename &#34;$key_path&#34;)&#34; = &#34;$(basename &#34;$key_path&#34; .pub)&#34; ]; then
                return 0
            fi
        fi
    done
    return 1
}

# Check SSH key file permissions
check_ssh_key_permissions() {
    local has_permission_issues=0
    
    for key in &#34;$HOME/.ssh&#34;/id_*; do
        # Skip if file doesn&#39;t exist or is a .pub file
        if [ ! -f &#34;$key&#34; ] || [ &#34;$key&#34; != &#34;${key%.pub}&#34; ]; then
            continue
        fi
        
        # Check permissions (works on both macOS and Linux)
        if command_exists stat; then
            # Try macOS format first, fall back to Linux
            perms=$(stat -f &#34;%Lp&#34; &#34;$key&#34; 2&gt;/dev/null || stat -c &#34;%a&#34; &#34;$key&#34; 2&gt;/dev/null || echo &#34;unknown&#34;)
            if [ &#34;$perms&#34; != &#34;600&#34; ] &amp;&amp; [ &#34;$perms&#34; != &#34;unknown&#34; ]; then
                print_message &#34;warning&#34; &#34;SSH key $key has permissions $perms (should be 600)&#34;
                echo &#34;  Fix with: chmod 600 &#39;$key&#39;&#34;
                has_permission_issues=1
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    return $has_permission_issues
}

# Check if SSH agent is running and has keys loaded
check_ssh_agent() {
    if [ -z &#34;${SSH_AUTH_SOCK:-}&#34; ]; then
        print_message &#34;warning&#34; &#34;SSH agent not detected&#34;
        echo &#34;  Start with: eval \&#34;\$(ssh-agent -s)\&#34;&#34;
        echo &#34;  Add key with: ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519&#34;
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if any keys are loaded
    if ! ssh-add -l &gt;/dev/null 2&gt;&amp;1; then
        print_message &#34;warning&#34; &#34;No SSH keys loaded in agent&#34;
        echo &#34;  Add key with: ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519&#34;
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}

# Print helpful error messages for permission denied
print_permission_denied_help() {
    local output=&#34;$1&#34;
    print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;SSH connection failed: Permission denied&#34;
    echo
    echo &#34;Common solutions (try in order):&#34;
    echo
    echo &#34;1. Add your SSH key to GitHub:&#34;
    echo &#34;   • Copy your public key:&#34;
    echo &#34;     pbcopy &lt; ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub&#34;
    echo &#34;   • Add it at: https://github.com/settings/ssh/new&#34;
    echo
    echo &#34;2. Start SSH agent and add your key:&#34;
    echo &#34;   • eval \&#34;\$(ssh-agent -s)\&#34;&#34;
    echo &#34;   • ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519&#34;
    echo
    echo &#34;3. Check SSH key permissions:&#34;
    echo &#34;   • chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519&#34;
    echo &#34;   • chmod 644 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub&#34;
    echo
    echo &#34;4. Test SSH key format:&#34;
    echo &#34;   • ssh-keygen -l -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519&#34;
    echo
    echo &#34;📚 Troubleshooting guide:&#34;
    echo &#34;   https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/troubleshooting-ssh/error-permission-denied-publickey&#34;
    echo
    echo &#34;Full SSH output:&#34;
    echo &#34;$output&#34;
}

# Print helpful error messages for network issues
print_network_error_help() {
    local output=&#34;$1&#34;
    print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;SSH connection failed: Network issue&#34;
    echo
    echo &#34;Possible causes:&#34;
    echo &#34;• No internet connection&#34;
    echo &#34;• DNS resolution issues&#34;
    echo &#34;• Corporate firewall blocking SSH (port 22)&#34;
    echo &#34;• VPN interfering with connections&#34;
    echo
    echo &#34;Try these steps:&#34;
    echo &#34;1. Check internet: ping -c 3 github.com&#34;
    echo &#34;2. Check DNS: nslookup github.com&#34;
    echo &#34;3. Try HTTPS instead of SSH for git operations&#34;
    echo
    echo &#34;Full SSH output:&#34;
    echo &#34;$output&#34;
}

# Print error message for unknown issues
print_unknown_error_help() {
    local output=&#34;$1&#34;
    print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;SSH connection failed with unexpected error&#34;
    echo
    echo &#34;This might be a temporary issue. Try:&#34;
    echo &#34;• Waiting a few minutes and running again&#34;
    echo &#34;• Checking GitHub status: https://www.githubstatus.com/&#34;
    echo &#34;• Running the test manually: ssh -T $GITHUB_SSH_HOST&#34;
    echo
    echo &#34;Full SSH output:&#34;
    echo &#34;$output&#34;
}

# Analyze SSH output and provide appropriate feedback
analyze_ssh_output() {
    local output=&#34;$1&#34;
    local exit_code=&#34;$2&#34;
    
    # GitHub returns exit code 1 for successful authentication (no shell access)
    if [ $exit_code -eq $SSH_SUCCESS_EXIT_CODE ] &amp;&amp; echo &#34;$output&#34; | grep -q &#34;successfully authenticated&#34;; then
        print_message &#34;success&#34; &#34;SSH connection to GitHub successful!&#34;
        echo &#34;$output&#34;
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for specific error patterns
    if echo &#34;$output&#34; | grep -q &#34;Permission denied&#34;; then
        print_permission_denied_help &#34;$output&#34;
    elif echo &#34;$output&#34; | grep -q &#34;Could not resolve hostname\|Name or service not known&#34;; then
        print_network_error_help &#34;$output&#34;
    elif echo &#34;$output&#34; | grep -q &#34;Connection timed out\|Connection refused&#34;; then
        print_network_error_help &#34;$output&#34;
    else
        print_unknown_error_help &#34;$output&#34;
    fi
    
    return 1
}

# Test SSH connection to GitHub
test_ssh_connection() {
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Testing SSH connection to GitHub...&#34;
    
    # Use timeout and connection timeout for better error handling
    local ssh_cmd=&#34;ssh -T -o ConnectTimeout=$SSH_CONNECT_TIMEOUT -o BatchMode=yes $GITHUB_SSH_HOST&#34;
    
    # Check if timeout command is available
    if command_exists timeout; then
        ssh_output=$(timeout &#34;$SSH_TIMEOUT&#34; $ssh_cmd 2&gt;&amp;1)
    elif command_exists gtimeout; then  # macOS with coreutils
        ssh_output=$(gtimeout &#34;$SSH_TIMEOUT&#34; $ssh_cmd 2&gt;&amp;1)
    else
        # Fallback without timeout
        print_message &#34;warning&#34; &#34;timeout command not available, SSH may hang&#34;
        ssh_output=$($ssh_cmd 2&gt;&amp;1)
    fi
    
    ssh_exit_code=$?
    
    # Handle timeout specifically
    if [ $ssh_exit_code -eq 124 ] || [ $ssh_exit_code -eq 142 ]; then
        print_message &#34;error&#34; &#34;SSH connection timed out after ${SSH_TIMEOUT}s&#34;
        echo &#34;This usually indicates network connectivity issues.&#34;
        return 1
    fi
    
    analyze_ssh_output &#34;$ssh_output&#34; $ssh_exit_code
}

# Main function
main() {
    print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Running SSH GitHub connectivity test (triggered by SSH key changes)&#34;
    
    # Check for SSH keys first
    if ! check_ssh_keys; then
        print_message &#34;skip&#34; &#34;No SSH keys found in ~/.ssh folder&#34;
        echo
        echo &#34;Generate SSH keys first:&#34;
        echo &#34;  ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C \&#34;your machine name\&#34;&#34;
        echo &#34;  ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C \&#34;your machine name\&#34;  # if ed25519 not supported&#34;
        exit 0
    fi
    
    # Check SSH key permissions (non-fatal)
    check_ssh_key_permissions || true
    
    # Check SSH agent (non-fatal)
    check_ssh_agent || true
    
    # Perform the actual SSH test
    if test_ssh_connection; then
        exit 0
    else
        exit 1
    fi
}

# Run main function with all arguments
main &#34;$@&#34;
{{ end -}}

{{ if eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34; -}}
#!/bin/bash

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

# Check required tools
if ! require_tools brew; then
    exit 1
fi

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Installing packages using Homebrew...&#34;

brew bundle --file=/dev/stdin &lt;&lt;EOF
{{ range .packages.darwin.taps -}}
tap {{ . | quote }}
{{ end -}}

# Always install core packages
{{ range .packages.darwin.core.brews -}}
brew {{ . | quote }}
{{ end -}}
{{ range .packages.darwin.core.casks -}}
cask {{ . | quote }}
{{ end -}}

{{- if has &#34;dev&#34; .tags }}
# Install dev packages
{{ range .packages.darwin.dev.brews -}}
brew {{ . | quote }}
{{ end -}}
{{ range .packages.darwin.dev.casks -}}
cask {{ . | quote }}
{{ end -}}
{{- end }}

{{- if has &#34;ai&#34; .tags }}
# Install AI packages
{{ range .packages.darwin.ai.brews -}}
brew {{ . | quote }}
{{ end -}}
{{ range .packages.darwin.ai.casks -}}
cask {{ . | quote }}
{{ end -}}
{{- end }}

{{- if has &#34;work&#34; .tags }}
# Install work packages
{{ range .packages.darwin.work.brews -}}
brew {{ . | quote }}
{{ end -}}
{{ range .packages.darwin.work.casks -}}
cask {{ . | quote }}
{{ end -}}
{{- end }}
EOF
{{ end -}}

{{- if eq .chezmoi.os &#34;darwin&#34; -}}
#!/bin/bash

# Source shared utility functions
source &#34;{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh&#34;

# Check required tools
if ! require_tools brew scutil; then
    exit 1
fi

# This script installs additional packages using Homebrew based on the specific computer name.
# 
# Maintenance: Update brewfiles by periodically running:
# brew bundle dump --file=$HOME/.brewfiles/current --force --force

{{ $computerName := includeTemplate &#34;computer-name&#34; . }}
{{- $brewfilePath := includeTemplate &#34;machine-brewfile-path&#34; (dict &#34;computerName&#34; $computerName &#34;chezmoi&#34; .chezmoi) -}}
{{- if $brewfilePath -}}
brewfile={{ $brewfilePath }}
# brewfile hash: {{ include $brewfilePath | sha256sum }}
{{- else -}}
brewfile=&#34;&#34;
{{- end }}

if [[ ! -f $brewfile ]]; then
    print_message &#34;skip&#34; &#34;Skipping additional package installation - brewfile not found for this machine ({{- $computerName -}})&#34;
    exit 0
fi

read -p &#34;🍺 Install additional packages from your brewfile? (y/N): &#34; -n 1 -r
echo
if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    print_message &#34;skip&#34; &#34;Skipping additional package installation&#34;
    exit 0
fi

print_message &#34;info&#34; &#34;Installing additional packages using Homebrew...&#34;

brew bundle -v --no-lock --file=$brewfile
{{ end -}}
--- End of content ---</code></pre>
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (gemini-2.5-pro 2025-09-16 02:00:36Z)**_

Of course. I've reviewed all the scripts in your `home/.chezmoiscripts/` directory.

Overall, this is an impressive and robust collection of scripts. They are well-structured, make excellent use of `chezmoi`'s features, and include great error handling and user feedback. The use of `run_onchange_*` scripts with hashes to detect changes is particularly well done.

I have a few minor suggestions for improvement:

### 1.  Use `jq` for parsing JSON in shell scripts

In `run_once_after_darwin-15-install-azul-zulu-jdk.sh.tmpl` and `run_once_before_darwin-35-install-nvm.sh.tmpl`, you use combinations of `grep`, `cut`, and `sed` to parse JSON from API responses. While this works, using `jq` would be more robust and readable. Since you are already using Homebrew, you could ensure `jq` is installed and then use it for parsing.

For example, in the JDK installation script, you could replace the complex `grep/sed` chain with:

```shell
download_url=$(echo "$response" | jq -r '.[0].download_url')
```

And in the `nvm` installation script:

```shell
NVM_VERSION=$(curl -s ... | jq -r '.tag_name')
```

### 2.  Avoid modifying shell rc files from scripts

In `run_once_before_darwin-30-install-uv.sh.tmpl`, you append the `uv` shell completion setup to `.bashrc` and `.zshrc`. It's generally better to manage shell configuration files like `.zshrc` entirely within `chezmoi` as templates.

Your `dot_zshrc.tmpl` already includes the necessary setup for `uv`. The script's modification could lead to duplicate entries. I'd recommend removing the `echo ... >> ~/.zshrc` lines from the `install-uv` script and relying on your `dot_zshrc.tmpl` file.

### 3.  Security of `curl | sh`

In `run_once_after_darwin-83-install-basic-memory.sh.tmpl` and a few other scripts, you use the `curl | sh` pattern to install software. While this is a common practice for many tools, it's worth remembering the security implications of running a script directly from the internet. Since these are your own dotfiles and you've chosen these tools, you've implicitly trusted the sources, which is perfectly reasonable.

These are minor points in what is an excellent set of installation and configuration scripts. Let me know if you'd like me to apply any of these changes.

---

_**Agent (gemini-2.5-pro 2025-09-16 02:39:22Z)**_

Based on your current setup, which is already quite powerful, here are a few other excellent Zsh plugins you might find useful, especially for development workflows.

### 1. `fzf` (Fuzzy Finder)

This is probably the single most impactful tool you could add. `fzf` is a general-purpose command-line fuzzy finder. When integrated with Zsh, it supercharges your shell history search (Ctrl+R), file searching, and more. It's incredibly fast and intuitive.

*   **Why?** It provides a much better experience for finding previous commands than the default history search.
*   **Installation:** `brew install fzf` and then add the `fzf` plugin to your `oh-my-zsh` plugins list or source its keybindings script.

### 2. `direnv`

I noticed you have an experimental, commented-out function in your `.zshrc` to auto-load `.env` files. `direnv` is a mature and robust tool that does exactly this and more. It allows you to have project-specific environment variables that are automatically loaded when you `cd` into a directory and unloaded when you leave.

*   **Why?** It's a secure and standard way to manage per-project environments, secrets, and configurations. It's a perfect fit for a developer.
*   **Installation:** `brew install direnv` and then add `eval "$(direnv hook zsh)"` to your `dot_zshrc.tmpl`.

### 3. `zoxide`

`zoxide` is a "smarter `cd` command". It remembers the directories you use most frequently, allowing you to jump to them with just a few keystrokes. For example, after navigating to `/Users/craig/projects/my-cool-project` a few times, you could just type `z cool` from anywhere to jump right back to it.

*   **Why?** It dramatically speeds up filesystem navigation.
*   **Installation:** `brew install zoxide` and add `eval "$(zoxide init zsh)"` to your `dot_zshrc.tmpl`.

### 4. `asdf-vm`

You are currently using `nvm` for Node.js and `jenv` for Java. `asdf` is a single, extensible version manager that can handle dozens of languages, including Node.js, Java, Python, Ruby, Rust, and more, all with a single CLI tool and a single configuration file (`.tool-versions`).

*   **Why?** It can simplify your toolchain by replacing multiple version managers (`nvm`, `jenv`) with a single one.
*   **Installation:** `brew install asdf` and then source it in your shell.

### A few others to consider:

*   **`atuin`**: Replaces your default shell history with a searchable, SQLite-powered, and optionally cloud-synced history. Given your multi-machine setup with `chezmoi`, you might appreciate having your shell history synced.
*   **`git-delta`**: Not a plugin, but a powerful diff viewer that makes `git diff` and `git log -p` much more readable. You can install it with `brew install git-delta` and configure it in your `.gitconfig`.

I would especially recommend looking at `fzf` and `direnv` as they would likely provide the most immediate benefits to your workflow.

Let me know if you're interested in any of these, and I can help you with the installation and configuration

_**Agent (gemini-2.5-pro 2025-09-16 02:42:41Z)**_

That's an excellent point. You are absolutely right that we should follow the established patterns in your dotfiles, and using `.chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl` is the perfect way you're managing self-contained Zsh plugins.

The recommendations I made (`fzf`, `direnv`, `zoxide`) are a bit different from the plugins you currently have, and this is a key distinction:

*   **Pure Zsh Plugins:** The plugins you have in `.chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl` (like `zsh-autosuggestions` and `fast-syntax-highlighting`) are collections of Zsh scripts. `chezmoi` downloads them into the right place, and then your `.zshrc` loads them. Your current method is perfect for this.

*   **Standalone CLI Tools with Shell Hooks:** Tools like `fzf`, `direnv`, and `zoxide` are actually compiled applications (written in Go or Rust). They are powerful command-line tools in their own right. The most common and reliable way to install and manage these applications on macOS is with Homebrew. The "plugin" part is just a small script or "hook" that you add to your `dot_zshrc.tmpl` to integrate the standalone tool with your shell.

So, the recommended workflow would be:

1.  **Install the application** using your existing Homebrew setup (e.g., by adding `fzf` to the `brews` list in your `packages.yaml`).
2.  **Activate the shell integration** by adding the appropriate line to your `dot_zshrc.tmpl` (e.g., `eval "$(zoxide init zsh)"`).

This approach respects your existing patterns: system packages and applications are handled by Homebrew, and your Zsh configuration is handled by `chezmoi` templates.

You could certainly install some of these tools (like `asdf`) via `git-repo` in your `.chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl`, but for compiled applications like `fzf` and `direnv`, using Homebrew is the standard and most straightforward path.
